items.question_text: Under 8-6-1, temporary stationary airspace reservations must be coordinated through appropriate _____ before activation.
items.explanation: Per 8-6-1, airspace reservations require coordination with appropriate ATS authorities.

items.question_text: Under 8-6-1, may an aircraft enter a stationary airspace reservation without ATC clearance?
items.explanation: Per 8-6-1, aircraft require ATC clearance to enter an active airspace reservation.

choices.text: Only if the aircraft is VFR and the reservation appears inactive.
Yes — any aircraft may enter if the reservation has been published in a NOTAM.
No — ATC clearance is required to enter an active airspace reservation.

items.question_text: Under 8-6-2, if a pilot refuses an avoidance clearance, the controller must advise the pilot of the _____ and request the pilot's intentions.
items.explanation: Per 8-6-2, the controller advises of the hazard or reason for the clearance and requests intentions.

items.question_text: Under 8-6-2, who bears responsibility when a pilot refuses an avoidance clearance for an airspace reservation?
items.explanation: Per 8-6-2, when a pilot refuses an avoidance clearance, the controller advises of the hazard and the pilot assumes responsibility for any consequences of the refusal.

choices.text: The controller bears full responsibility regardless of the pilot's decision.
Responsibility is shared equally between controller and pilot.
The pilot assumes responsibility after being advised of the hazard.
The flight's operator bears all responsibility.

items.question_text: Under 8-6-3, how does a temporary moving airspace reservation differ from a stationary one?
items.explanation: Per 8-6-3, moving airspace reservations travel with the reserved activity (such as military operations) and require separation along the moving path rather than at a fixed location.

choices.text: Moving reservations only apply above FL180.
Moving reservations require no coordination while stationary ones do.
Moving reservations travel with an activity along a path, while stationary reservations are tied to a fixed geographic area.
There is no difference — both are treated identically under Chapter 8.
